# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-07-17

A loophole let Claude's web_fetch follow attacker-embedded URLs from previously-fetched pages, enabling data exfiltration.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. How I tricked Claude into leaking your deepest, darkest secrets
   Concrete, reproducible agent-exfiltration vector that directly informs how you wire fetch tools with private context.

2. Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model
   Open-weights model releases change the local-model evaluation set for solo builders.

3. Test out the Neon backend: Object Storage, Functions, and AI Gateway are beta
   One-API/one-bill multi-provider AI gateway plus agent-declared backend is meaningful leverage for solo agent work.

4. xai-org/grok-build, now open source
   A real supply-chain/privacy failure in a popular agent CLI plus the mitigation and open-source response — directly informs agent-CLI trust decisions.

5. v0.32.1
   Directly relevant to a local-Ollama agent setup; the cache-leak fix affects long-running memory use.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-07-17
